description: Routing corpus for the plugin lifecycle tool (author/carve/edit + score/critique/promote). Triggers cover building, carving, packaging, and evaluating Claude Code plugins + marketplaces. Adversarials route to the siblings that own adjacent layers: skill content (skills-studio), skill restructuring (skills-refactor), MCP tool design (core-mcp-best-practices), agent loops (core-agent-loops), domain-expert skills (meta-expert-author), app scaffolds (meta-app-scaffold).
trigger_phrases: package these skills into a plugin
author a Claude Code plugin for our design system
carve our skill library into coherent plugins
what plugins should these skills become?
should this be a plugin or a standalone skill?
should this be a plugin, a skill, or an MCP?
build a marketplace.json for our plugins
score this plugin against best practices
is this plugin well-bundled and ready to ship?
run the critic panel on this plugin
what components should this plugin bundle — hooks, skills, agents?
how should I split these capabilities into plugins?
is this plugin a kitchen sink?
review our plugin marketplace before we publish
adversarial_phrases: create a skill for writing DB migrations
rename core-foo to foo and fix the references
merge these two skills into one
design the tool schemas for our MCP server
design the agent loop / orchestration for this goal
build a comprehensive domain-expert skill like ref-dashboard
scaffold a new reference app under apps/
notes: Adversarials route to siblings that stay separate: authoring/scoring one skill's content -> skills-studio; renaming/merging/retiring/splitting skills -> skills-refactor; a single MCP server's tool schemas/perimeter -> core-mcp-best-practices; agent-loop orchestration -> core-agent-loops; a domain-expert knowledge skill -> meta-expert-author; an apps/ reference-app scaffold (borrows the plugin layout but is not a distributable plugin) -> meta-app-scaffold. plugins-studio owns the plugin bundle: whether to package, what to bundle, how to share legally, and whether the bundle is well-architected.
